name: El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail
name_original: Sierra de las Nieves – circular por el río Turón y El Burgo
description: This moderate 14.4-mile (23.1 km) mountain bike loop near El Burgo offers 1855 feet (565 metres) of climbing through Sierra de las Nieves.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around El Burgo traverse the diverse landscapes of Andalusia, offering routes within and adjacent to the Sierra de las Nieves National Park. The region features varied terrain, from challenging mountain passes like Puerto del Viento to shaded pine forests and paths alongside the Río de El Burgo. Riders can explore routes that include significant elevation gains and scenic viewpoints. Best mountain bike trails around El Burgo The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra de las Nieves – circular por el río Turón y El Burgo, a moderate 14.4 miles (23.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This circular route offers varied terrain through the Sierra de las Nieves and along the Turón River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Burgo Village Center – El Burgo loop from El Burgo, a difficult 33.2 miles (53.4 km) path. This extensive loop features significant elevation gain, starting and ending in the village center. Local mountain bikers also love the El Burgo – Entrance bridge to El Burgo loop from Yunquera, a 29.2 miles (47.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Mountain biking around El Burgo is defined by the rugged Sierra de las Nieves National Park, extensive pine forests, and routes along the Río de El Burgo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with substantial climbs. The routes in El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Burgo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Burgo?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails around El Burgo, offering a diverse range of options for mountain bikers. These include 23 moderate routes and 21 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Burgo?
A: The terrain around El Burgo is varied, featuring the rugged landscapes of the Sierra de las Nieves National Park, extensive pine forests, and paths alongside the Río de El Burgo. You'll encounter everything from comfortable, often shaded unpaved roads to challenging mountain passes with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting and ending in El Burgo?
A: Yes, El Burgo is an excellent starting point for circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994285226/sierra-de-las-nieves-circular-por-el-rio-turon-y-el-burgo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de las Nieves – circular por el río Turón y El Burgo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 23.1 km loop that takes you through varied terrain and along the Turón River. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo Village Center – El Burgo loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, an extensive 53.4 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in El Burgo?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by interesting landmarks. You can experience the breathtaking scenery from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wind Pass&lt;/a&gt; (Puerto del Viento), which offers panoramic views of the Sierra de las Nieves and Sierra de Grazalema. The picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5339438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Ventilla&lt;/a&gt;, a historic stone bridge, is another point of interest. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/613806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrecilla Peak (1,919 m)&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic backdrop and can be seen from various points.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Burgo?
A: El Burgo is well-suited for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. The region's diverse landscapes and extensive pine forests provide shaded routes, which can be particularly pleasant during warmer months. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in El Burgo?
A: While many routes in El Burgo feature significant elevation and challenging sections, there are options for less experienced riders. The region offers a mix of difficulties, including some moderate trails. When planning a family outing, look for routes described as 'moderate' and consider their length and elevation profiles to ensure they match your family's skill level.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Burgo?
A: The mountain bike trails around El Burgo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the opportunity to explore the beautiful Sierra de las Nieves National Park.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes with significant elevation gain in El Burgo?
A: Absolutely. El Burgo is known for its challenging routes, especially those traversing the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de las Nieves. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14593436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Entrance bridge to El Burgo loop from Yunquera&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.1 km trail with over 1,190 meters of elevation gain, often completed in about 4 hours. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10296901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo – Entrance bridge to El Burgo loop from Serrato&lt;/a&gt;, a 62.6 km route with over 1,430 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there options for mountain biking through pine forests in El Burgo?
A: Yes, the area around El Burgo is characterized by extensive pine forests, particularly around the Monumento Natural Mirador del Guarda Forestal. Many mountain bike routes, including circular ones, traverse these superb forest trails, providing shade and a scenic riding experience.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay in El Burgo after a ride?
A: El Burgo is a historic town that offers amenities for visitors. You'll find local cafes and pubs where you can refuel after your ride. The town also provides accommodation options, allowing you to combine your outdoor adventure with cultural exploration of its historical attractions like the Castillo Miraflores and the Church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación.

Q: Is it possible to reach mountain bike trails in El Burgo using public transport?
A: While El Burgo is a rural town, it is generally accessible. However, specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to El Burgo and plan your route accordingly, as many trails start directly from or near the village center.

Q: Where can I park my car when mountain biking in El Burgo?
A: Parking is typically available within the village of El Burgo itself. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo Village Center – El Burgo loop from El Burgo&lt;/a&gt;, start and end in the village center, making it convenient to park your vehicle before heading out on the trails.
tips: The origin of this town is Celtic, but in its streets you can see vestiges of its Roman and Andalusian past and as a refuge for bandits from the Costa del Sol. It has been rebuilt on several occasions, mainly after the 1775 earthquake that caused it very affected.

This trail makes up the first stage between Ronda and El Burgo of the Long Distance Trail number 243, whose commercial name is Sendero de la Sierra de las Nieves. It is mainly used by hikers so drive carefully in the less wide sections.
This port is a mandatory stop both to rest and to contemplate the foothills of Sierra Hidalga and its centuries-old Spanish firs.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike route is rated as moderate. This means you should have good fitness and some advanced riding skills. Expect varied terrain, including unpaved segments, and be prepared for some technical sections where you might need to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain around El Burgo, where this trail is located, typically features a mix of forest paths and rural roads. You'll encounter hilly landscapes and unpaved segments. While specific details for the 'El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail' are limited, routes in this region can include loose stone and require advanced riding skills in places.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near the village of El Burgo. You can usually find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially near the El Burgo Village Center.

faqs.question: Is the El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in Sierra de las Nieves National Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike in the Sierra de las Nieves region, including this trail, is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potential for colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are no general entrance fees to access Sierra de las Nieves National Park. However, certain activities or specific areas within the park might require permits, especially for larger groups or organized events. For individual mountain biking, it's usually not required, but it's always good practice to check the official park website for the latest regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: This loop trail offers a chance to experience the natural beauty and cultural landscape of the region. You'll pass through the charming El Burgo Village Center. The route also includes a section of the GR 243 Trail Section – Sierra de las Nieves and goes by Puerto de la Mujer, offering scenic views of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll ride along parts of the El Burgo - Ronda, El Burgo - La Fuensanta, and the GR 243. It also uses segments of Carril del Dique - Pista Fuensanta, los sauces, and Sendero Paco Arjona, among others, providing a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat in El Burgo after the ride?
faqs.answer: Yes, El Burgo is a traditional Spanish village and offers several options for refreshments. You'll find local cafes and restaurants in the El Burgo Village Center where you can enjoy a meal or a drink after your mountain biking adventure.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is essential, and gloves are highly recommended. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also advisable, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach El Burgo?
faqs.answer: While El Burgo is a smaller village, it is generally accessible by regional bus services from larger towns in the Málaga province. However, public transport options might be limited, especially on weekends or holidays. It's best to check current schedules with local transport providers like spain.info or local bus companies for the most up-to-date information.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the El Burgo - Puerto de la Mujer Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers inspiring views of the cultural landscape and natural beauty of the Sierra de las Nieves region. You can expect picturesque scenery with lush forests, rolling hills, and elevated positions providing panoramic vistas. The area is known for its diverse landscapes, characteristic of the Andalusian interior.

waypoints.name: GR 243 Trail Section – Sierra de las Nieves
